questions.prompt: Complete with agreement: 'Ela tem os olhos ___.'
questions.explanation: azul → azuis (like animal → animais); olhos is plural, so the adjective is too.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ele escreve com as duas ___.' (hands)
questions.explanation: mão just adds -s: mão → mãos.

questions.prompt: Full agreement: 'As casas da aldeia são ___.' (white)
questions.explanation: casas = feminine plural → brancas.

questions.prompt: Put in order: praise Portugal's famous custard tarts.
questions.explanation: pastel → pastéis (-l → -éis); deliciosos agrees in masculine plural.

activities.title: A fotografia de família
activities.intro: Describing people in a photo — plurals and agreement everywhere. Read aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quem são estas pessoas na fotografia?
lines.en: Who are these people in the photo?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: São os meus irmãos. Eles são altos e morenos.
lines.en: They're my brothers. They are tall and dark-haired.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as duas raparigas ao lado?
lines.en: And the two girls next to them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: São as minhas primas. São muito simpáticas.
lines.en: They're my cousins. They are very nice.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Têm os olhos azuis, como tu.
lines.en: They have blue eyes, like you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim! E na mesa há pães e limões — foi um almoço de família.
lines.en: Yes! And on the table there are bread rolls and lemons — it was a family lunch.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que fotografia bonita!
lines.en: What a lovely photo!

days.title: Profissões
days.objective: Say what you do (Sou enfermeira — no article!), ask 'Onde trabalhas?' and name common jobs and workplaces.
days.paretoFocus: Unlike English, professions take NO article: 'Sou professor', never 'Sou um professor'. Learn that one rule plus ten job words and you can survive any introduction.
drills: Say your own job and those of 3 people you know: 'Sou…, ele é…'.
Ask and answer aloud: 'Onde trabalhas?' — 'Trabalho num…'.
Review today's job words in the Words tab.

items: Recall: article or no article with professions?
Say 'waiter' the European Portuguese way.
Name 3 workplaces with the right preposition (no hospital, na escola…).

activities.title: O que fazes?
activities.intro: Jobs and workplaces — and the golden rule: no article before the profession.

items.en: I'm a nurse — NO article in Portuguese: never 'Sou uma enfermeira'

items.hr: o médico / a médica, o professor / a professora
items.en: doctor, teacher — most jobs have a feminine form

items.hr: o empregado / a empregada de mesa
items.en: waiter / waitress — the European Portuguese term

items.hr: o engenheiro, a advogada, o cozinheiro
items.en: engineer, lawyer, cook
Where do you work? — I work in an office. (num = em + um)

items.hr: no hospital, na escola, na fábrica, numa loja
items.en: at the hospital, at the school, at the factory, in a shop
to be unemployed / retired ('reformado' is the pt-PT word for retired)

activities.title: Trabalho e profissões
activities.intro: Choose or complete.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct in Portuguese?
questions.explanation: Professions take no article: Sou professor / Sou médica.

questions.prompt: The person who serves you in a Portuguese restaurant is…
questions.explanation: Empregado/empregada de mesa = waiter/waitress in European Portuguese.

questions.prompt: 'A médica trabalha…' — pick the natural workplace.
questions.explanation: A doctor works at the hospital: no hospital (em + o).

questions.prompt: 'A fábrica' means…
questions.explanation: Falso amigo: fábrica = factory, not fabric (which is 'tecido').

questions.prompt: Ask a friend where they work: 'Onde ___?' (you work, tu form)
questions.explanation: tu trabalhas — 2nd person singular of trabalhar.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ela é ___ e trabalha no hospital.' (nurse, feminine — no article!)
questions.explanation: Ela é enfermeira — profession without article.

questions.prompt: Put in order: say where your brother works.
questions.explanation: O meu irmão trabalha num escritório em Lisboa. — num = em + um.

activities.title: E tu, o que fazes?
activities.intro: Two new acquaintances talk about their jobs. Read both parts aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, onde trabalhas?
lines.en: So, where do you work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trabalho num hospital. Sou enfermeiro. E tu?
lines.en: I work in a hospital. I'm a nurse. And you?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sou professora numa escola em Braga.
lines.en: I'm a teacher at a school in Braga.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gostas do teu trabalho?
lines.en: Do you like your job?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gosto muito. Os alunos são simpáticos. E o teu horário é difícil?
lines.en: I like it a lot. The students are nice. And is your schedule hard?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Às vezes trabalho de noite, mas gosto de ajudar as pessoas.
lines.en: Sometimes I work nights, but I like helping people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que bom! É um trabalho importante.
lines.en: How nice! It's an important job.

days.title: Tempos livres
days.objective: Talk about hobbies and weekends: jogar à bola, tocar guitarra, ler, dar um passeio — and what you like doing.
days.paretoFocus: Two verbs split the hobby world: jogar for games and sports, tocar for instruments. Add 'gostar de + infinitive' and you can discuss any pastime.
drills: List your 3 favourite hobbies aloud with 'Gosto de…'.
Say what you do 'ao fim de semana'.
Review today's words in the Words tab.

items: Recall: jogar or tocar for the guitar?
Say 'I play football with my friends' the pt-PT way.
How do the Portuguese say 'at the weekend'?

activities.title: O que fazes nos tempos livres?
activities.intro: Hobby verbs and weekend vocabulary — with the very Portuguese 'ao fim de semana'.

items.en: to play football — 'jogar à bola' is the everyday pt-PT way to say it
to play an instrument — always tocar, never jogar
to read / to watch TV — 'Vejo televisão à noite.'
to go for a walk — 'Dou um passeio à beira-mar.' (by the sea)

items.hr: o fim de semana — ao fim de semana
items.en: the weekend — 'ao fim de semana' is how pt-PT says 'at the weekend'

items.hr: ir à praia / ir ao café
items.en: to go to the beach / to the café — classic Portuguese weekend plans

items.hr: gostar de + infinitivo — Gosto de ler.
items.en: to like doing something: gostar de + infinitive

activities.title: Passatempos
activities.intro: Choose or complete.

questions.prompt: For musical instruments, Portuguese uses the verb…
questions.explanation: tocar guitarra, tocar piano; jogar is for games and sports.

questions.prompt: Complete the everyday pt-PT expression: 'jogar ___ bola'.
questions.explanation: jogar à bola = to play football (informal, very common in Portugal).

questions.prompt: 'Ver televisão' means…
questions.explanation: In pt-PT you 'see' television: ver televisão.

questions.prompt: How does European Portuguese usually say 'at the weekend'?
questions.explanation: 'Ao fim de semana vou à praia.' — a very Portuguese turn of phrase.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ao fim de semana gosto de ___ um passeio.' (to go for / to take)
questions.explanation: dar um passeio = to go for a walk.

questions.prompt: Ask a friend: '___ de ler?' (do you like, tu form)
questions.explanation: tu gostas — 'Gostas de ler?' = Do you like reading?

questions.prompt: Put in order: say what you do on Saturdays.
questions.explanation: Aos sábados jogo à bola com os meus amigos. = On Saturdays I play football with my friends.

activities.title: Os teus passatempos
activities.intro: Two friends compare hobbies. Read both parts aloud, then just the 'Me' lines.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O que fazes nos tempos livres?
lines.en: What do you do in your free time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jogo à bola com os meus amigos e toco guitarra.
lines.en: I play football with my friends and I play the guitar.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tocas bem?
lines.en: Do you play well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mais ou menos! E tu, o que gostas de fazer?
lines.en: More or less! And you, what do you like doing?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gosto de ler e de dar passeios à beira-mar.
lines.en: I like reading and going for walks by the sea.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E ao fim de semana?
lines.en: And at the weekend?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ao fim de semana vou à praia com a família.
lines.en: At the weekend I go to the beach with my family.

days.title: Combinar planos
days.objective: Make plans the Portuguese way: invite with 'Queres ir…?', feel like things with 'Apetece-me…', and seal the deal with 'Combinado!'.
days.paretoFocus: Three fixed replies carry every plan: 'Pode ser' (works for me), 'Combinado!' (deal!) and 'Fica para sábado' (let's make it Saturday). Add apetecer and you sound native.
drills: Invite an imaginary friend to 3 different places with 'Queres ir…?'.
Say 3 things you feel like right now: 'Apetece-me…'.
Review today's words in the Words tab.

items: Recall the three deal-making replies: Pode ser / Combinado / Fica para…
How does apetecer work — who is the subject?
Invite someone to dinner on Friday, out loud.

activities.title: Fazer planos
activities.intro: The building blocks of every Portuguese plan — invitations, cravings and confirmations.

items.en: Do you want to go to the cinema? — the standard informal invitation
Sounds good / works for me — the flexible yes
It's a deal! / Agreed! — seals the plan

items.hr: Apetece-me um café. / Apetece-te ir jantar fora?
items.en: I feel like a coffee. / Do you feel like eating out? — apetecer works like 'to appeal to me'
Let's make it Saturday. — literally 'it stays for Saturday'

items.hr: A que horas? — Às oito e meia.
items.en: At what time? — At half past eight.
to set/schedule — Let's set it for Friday.

activities.title: Combinado!
activities.intro: Choose or complete.

questions.prompt: Your friend proposes a time and place and you agree to seal the plan. You say…
questions.explanation: Combinado! = it's a deal, we're on.

questions.prompt: 'Apetece-me um gelado' means…
options: I feel like an ice cream
I already ate an ice cream
questions.answer: I feel like an ice cream
questions.explanation: apetecer + indirect pronoun: Apetece-me = it appeals to me = I feel like.

questions.prompt: 'Pode ser' is closest to…
questions.explanation: A relaxed, very Portuguese way of accepting a suggestion.

questions.prompt: You can't make it today, so you postpone: '___ para sábado.'
questions.explanation: Fica para sábado = let's leave it for Saturday.

questions.prompt: Invite a friend: '___ ir jantar fora?' (do you want, tu form)
questions.explanation: Queres ir jantar fora? = Do you want to go out for dinner?

questions.prompt: Complete the pronoun: 'Apetece-___ um café.' (to ME)
questions.explanation: apetecer takes an indirect pronoun attached after the verb in pt-PT: apetece-me, apetece-te.

questions.prompt: Put in order: invite someone to the cinema on Saturday night.
questions.explanation: Queres ir ao cinema no sábado à noite? = Do you want to go to the cinema on Saturday night?

activities.title: Vamos combinar
activities.intro: Making a Saturday plan, start to finish. Read both parts aloud.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Olá! Queres ir ao cinema no sábado?
lines.en: Hi! Do you want to go to the cinema on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa ideia! A que horas?
lines.en: Good idea! At what time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Às nove da noite. Pode ser?
lines.en: At nine in the evening. Does that work?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode ser. E antes, apetece-me jantar fora.
lines.en: Works for me. And beforehand, I feel like eating out.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então jantamos às sete e meia no restaurante ao lado do cinema.
lines.en: Then we'll have dinner at half past seven at the restaurant next to the cinema.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfeito. Combinado!
lines.en: Perfect. It's a deal!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado! Até sábado.
lines.en: Deal! See you Saturday.

days.title: Ao telefone
days.objective: Handle a phone call in Portugal: answer with 'Está lá?', leave a message (deixar recado) and use your telemóvel vocabulary.
days.paretoFocus: Five verbs run every call: ligar, atender, deixar recado, mandar uma mensagem, desligar. And the phone is a 'telemóvel' — the everyday pt-PT word.
drills: Answer 3 imaginary calls aloud: 'Estou? / Está lá?'.
Leave a pretend message: 'Diga-lhe que ligou o/a…'.
Review today's phone words in the Words tab.

items: Recall the pt-PT word for mobile phone.
What do the Portuguese say when they pick up the phone?
Recall: atender vs desligar — which is which?

activities.title: O telemóvel
activities.intro: Phone vocabulary the European way — starting with the word for the phone itself.

items.en: the mobile phone — the everyday word in Portugal
Hello? — what the Portuguese say when answering the phone
to answer (the phone) — 'Ela não atendeu.' (She didn't pick up.)
to call someone — 'Ligo-te mais tarde.' (I'll call you later.)
to leave a message (with a person) — 'Quer deixar recado?'
to send a text message — 'Manda-me uma mensagem.'
to hang up / the number — 'Qual é o teu número?'

activities.title: Estou?
activities.intro: Choose or complete.

questions.prompt: The everyday word for 'mobile phone' in Portugal is…
questions.explanation: Telemóvel = mobile phone in European Portuguese; o telefone fixo is the landline.

questions.prompt: A Portuguese person picks up the phone and says…
questions.explanation: 'Estou?', 'Está?' or 'Está lá?' are the standard phone greetings in Portugal.

questions.prompt: 'Atender' means…
questions.explanation: atender = to pick up/answer; desligar = to hang up.

questions.prompt: At the end of the call you…
questions.explanation: desligar = to hang up; ligar = to call; atender = to pick up.

questions.prompt: He didn't pick up: 'Ele não atendeu. Vou deixar ___.' (a spoken message with a person)
questions.explanation: deixar recado = to leave a message with someone.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não posso falar agora — vou ___ uma mensagem.' (to send)
questions.explanation: mandar uma mensagem = to send a text.

questions.prompt: Put in order: ask them to call you later.
questions.explanation: Liga-me mais tarde, por favor. — the pronoun attaches after the verb in pt-PT.

activities.title: Quer deixar recado?
activities.intro: You call Ana, but someone else picks up. Read both parts aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está lá?
lines.en: Hello? (answering the phone)

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Estou a falar com a Ana?
lines.en: Good afternoon. Am I speaking with Ana?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não, a Ana não está. Quer deixar recado?
lines.en: No, Ana isn't in. Do you want to leave a message?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, faz favor. Diga-lhe que ligou o Miguel.
lines.en: Yes, please. Tell her that Miguel called.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. Ela tem o seu número de telemóvel?
lines.en: Certainly. Does she have your mobile number?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tem, sim. Ou pode mandar-me uma mensagem.
lines.en: Yes, she does. Or she can send me a text.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem, eu dou-lhe o recado.
lines.en: All right, I'll give her the message.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito obrigado. Boa tarde!
lines.en: Thank you very much. Good afternoon!

days.title: Revisão A1 II
days.objective: Consolidate the last eight days — transport, formality, demonstratives, plurals, jobs, hobbies, plans and phone calls — and put it all to work at the market.
days.paretoFocus: Review beats new material: re-activating these eight themes now locks them in. The market dialogue recombines demonstratives, plurals and polite register in one real scene.
drills: Speed round: say one sentence from each of the last 8 days.
Role-play the market dialogue aloud from the 'Me' side only.
Review this week's word list in the Words tab.

items: Recall: apanhar vs perder o autocarro.
Switch one question between tu and o senhor register.
Pluralise pão, limão and mão from memory.

activities.title: Oito dias num relance
activities.intro: One anchor per theme. If any line feels shaky, revisit that day before the checkpoint.

items.hr: Apanho o autocarro na paragem. / Perdi o comboio!
items.en: transport: catch the bus at the stop / I missed the train!
register: the verb ending switches the formality
the demonstrative triangle: my space, your space, far from both

items.hr: pão → pães, limão → limões, mão → mãos
items.en: the three -ão plurals, one example each
professions with NO article + workplace with num/numa

items.hr: Jogo à bola e toco guitarra ao fim de semana.
items.en: hobbies: jogar for sports, tocar for instruments
making plans: invitation, flexible yes, sealed deal

items.hr: Está lá? — ligar, atender, deixar recado
items.en: phone: hello?, to call, to pick up, to leave a message

activities.title: Tudo misturado
activities.intro: Mixed questions from lessons 1-8 of this block.

questions.prompt: The European Portuguese word for 'tram' is…
questions.explanation: O elétrico = the tram; o comboio = the train.

questions.prompt: Formal version of 'Tens troco?' (Do you have change?) to a shopkeeper:
questions.explanation: Formal register: o senhor + 3rd person verb.

questions.prompt: The plural of 'pão' is…
questions.explanation: pão → pães, like cão → cães.

questions.prompt: 'Apetece-me um café' means…
questions.explanation: apetecer + me = it appeals to me = I feel like.

questions.prompt: Ask about the thing in your friend's hand: '___ é isso?' (What)
questions.explanation: O que é isso…? — isso because it is in YOUR listener's hand.

questions.prompt: Complete (profession, feminine, no article): 'O meu pai é médico e a minha mãe é ___.' (teacher)
questions.explanation: Ela é professora — no article before professions.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Vou apanhar o ___ para a Baixa.' (the bus)
questions.explanation: apanhar o autocarro — the pt-PT word for bus, always.

questions.prompt: Put in order: offer a choice between a message with you or a text.
questions.explanation: Queres deixar recado ou mandas uma mensagem? — two phone options, tu register.

activities.title: No mercado
activities.intro: Shopping at the market — demonstratives, plurals and polite register all working together. Read both parts aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia! Diga, faz favor.
lines.en: Good morning! What can I get you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Quanto custam estas laranjas?
lines.en: Good morning. How much are these oranges?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Essas custam um euro e vinte o quilo.
lines.en: Those cost one euro twenty a kilo.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então queria dois quilos, e também aqueles limões ali.
lines.en: Then I'd like two kilos, and also those lemons over there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com certeza. Mais alguma coisa?
lines.en: Certainly. Anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dois pães, se faz favor.
lines.en: Two bread rolls, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Aqui tem. São quatro euros e cinquenta.
lines.en: Here you are. That's four euros fifty.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aqui está. Obrigado, bom dia!
lines.en: Here you go. Thank you, good day!

days.title: Ponto de controlo A1
days.objective: A1 checkpoint in the style of CAPLE ACESSO/CIPLE tasks: read short authentic notices, answer comprehension questions, fill in a form and ask for information at the junta de freguesia — a rehearsal for the CIPLE exam path.
days.paretoFocus: CAPLE exams test exactly this: understanding public notices, filling in personal-data forms and handling a service-desk conversation. Practise the task types, not just the words.
resources: CAPLE — Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
drills: Read the three notices aloud and summarise each in one English sentence.
Fill in an imaginary form aloud: nome, apelido, morada, data de nascimento.
Role-play the junta de freguesia dialogue from the 'Me' side.

items: Recall the form fields: what do apelido and morada mean?
What does 'encerrado' mean on a notice?
Recall: 2.ª a 6.ª — which days are these?

activities.title: Três avisos autênticos
activities.intro: CIPLE-style reading: three short public texts of the kind you meet on walls, doors and noticeboards in Portugal. Read each, then check the key words.

items.hr: AVISO — Metro encerrado entre as 10h e as 12h por obras. Use o autocarro 728.
items.en: NOTICE — Metro closed between 10:00 and 12:00 due to works. Use bus 728.
key words: closed (formal notice word) / due to (building) works

items.hr: HORÁRIO — Junta de Freguesia: 2.ª a 6.ª, das 9h às 17h. Encerrado ao fim de semana.
items.en: OPENING HOURS — Parish office: Mon to Fri, 9:00 to 17:00. Closed at the weekend.
key abbreviation: Portuguese notices number the weekdays — 2.ª (Monday) to 6.ª (Friday)

items.hr: ANÚNCIO — Vende-se bicicleta, 50 €. Contactar: 912 345 678, depois das 18h.
items.en: AD — Bicycle for sale, 50 €. Contact: 912 345 678, after 18:00.
key words: for sale (literally 'sells itself') / to contact

activities.title: Compreensão e formulário
activities.intro: Part 1: comprehension questions on the three notices. Part 2: fill in the form fields, CIPLE-style.

questions.prompt: Segundo o aviso, o metro está encerrado…
questions.explanation: The notice says: 'encerrado entre as 10h e as 12h por obras'.

questions.prompt: Durante as obras no metro, o que deve usar?
questions.explanation: 'Use o autocarro 728.' — the alternative given in the notice.

questions.prompt: A junta de freguesia está aberta…
questions.explanation: 2.ª a 6.ª = de segunda a sexta-feira; 'encerrado ao fim de semana'.

questions.prompt: No anúncio, 'encerrado' e 'fechado' são… — e quem quer a bicicleta deve ligar…
questions.explanation: encerrado = fechado (closed); the ad says 'Contactar… depois das 18h'.

questions.prompt: Formulário — Nome: Ana. ___: Silva. (the field asking for the family name)
questions.explanation: Apelido = surname/family name in European Portuguese forms.

questions.prompt: Formulário — ___: Rua das Flores, 12, Lisboa. (the field asking where you live)
questions.explanation: Morada = address, the standard pt-PT form field.

questions.prompt: Formulário — Data de ___: 03/05/1999. (the field asking when you were born)
questions.explanation: Data de nascimento = date of birth.

questions.prompt: Put in order: ask politely what documents are needed.
questions.explanation: Que documentos são precisos, faz favor? — a polite service-desk question.

activities.title: Na junta de freguesia
activities.intro: Asking for information at the parish office — a classic CIPLE interaction task. Read both parts aloud, then just the 'Me' lines.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Preciso de uma informação, faz favor.
lines.en: Good morning. I need some information, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia. Diga.
lines.en: Good morning. Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Queria um atestado de residência. O que é preciso?
lines.en: I'd like a certificate of residence. What is needed?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Precisa do cartão de cidadão e de preencher um formulário.
lines.en: You need your citizen card and to fill in a form.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tenho de marcar hora ou posso esperar?
lines.en: Do I have to book a time or can I wait?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode esperar. Escreva aqui o nome, o apelido e a morada.
lines.en: You can wait. Write your first name, surname and address here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Com certeza. Quanto tempo demora?
lines.en: Of course. How long does it take?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cerca de quinze minutos. Sente-se, faz favor.
lines.en: About fifteen minutes. Please take a seat.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito obrigado.
lines.en: Thank you very much.
